title: Pyrus guanii

If you are referring to "Pyrus communis", here's a paragraph about it:

Pyrus communis, also known as the European Pear, is a deciduous tree species belonging to the family Rosaceae. The health benefits of Pyrus communis include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and protect against certain diseases. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus communis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus communis include tea, extracts, and infusions, which are used to support digestive health and relieve symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders.

Pyrus pashia, also known as the Assam pear, is a deciduous tree species belonging to the family Rosaceae.
